title: First source and main

canonicalExplanation.summary: A conventional Java application places a correctly named class in a source file, declares a supported static main entry point, compiles it, and launches the resulting class by binary name.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Main.java declares public class Main with public static void main(String[] args), prints one line, compiles with javac, and runs with java Main. Your goal is to build and explain a minimal Java application from the command line. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: pf computing execution; pf tools terminal editor; JDK, JVM and Java language. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A conventional Java application places a correctly named class in a source file, declares a supported static main entry point, compiles it, and launches the resulting class by binary name. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Source-file launch modes and newer compact entry forms exist, but a beginner must still understand class names, file paths, compilation output, class paths, and process exit behavior. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Java executes any method named main regardless of its declaring class, modifiers, parameter type, or how the class is launched.

includes: Write, compile and run a source file with a conventional application entry point.
Build and explain a minimal Java application from the command line.
excludes: Source-file launch modes and newer compact entry forms exist, but a beginner must still understand class names, file paths, compilation output, class paths, and process exit behavior.

outcomes: Build and explain a minimal Java application from the command line.

examples.title: First source and main: direct language case
examples.situation: Main.java declares public class Main with public static void main(String[] args), prints one line, compiles with javac, and runs with java Main.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: Main.java declares public class Main with public static void main(String[] args), prints one line, compiles with javac, and runs with java Main.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: For the conventional entry contract, declare public static void main(String[] args) and launch the containing class by its binary name.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because For the conventional entry contract, declare public static void main(String[] args) and launch the containing class by its binary name.

examples.title: First source and main: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: The compiler rejects a public class named App stored in Main.java, so the learner repairs the file/class contract instead of changing the runtime command randomly.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: The compiler rejects a public class named App stored in Main.java, so the learner repairs the file/class contract instead of changing the runtime command randomly.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Source-file launch modes and newer compact entry forms exist, but a beginner must still understand class names, file paths, compilation output, class paths, and process exit behavior.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Java executes any method named main regardless of its declaring class, modifiers, parameter type, or how the class is launched.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Build and explain a minimal Java application from the command line.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Java executes any method named main regardless of its declaring class, modifiers, parameter type, or how the class is launched.
misconceptions.correction: For the conventional entry contract, declare public static void main(String[] args) and launch the containing class by its binary name.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Java executes any method named main regardless of its declaring class, modifiers, parameter type, or how the class is launched.
